title: Drone use warning issued by Glasgow Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The airport warned that using drones close to an airfield could endanger flights (Image: Jason Mavrommatis) Officials say there is a 5km restriction zone around the airport where drones must not be flown due to the potential risk to aircraft operations. The airport warned that using drones close to an airfield could endanger flights and may result in enforcement action.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Drone operators are also reminded to stay below 400ft (120m) and keep at least 150ft (around 50m) away from people and property. They are also urged never to fly near aircraft and to follow the UK’s Drone Safe Code when operating unmanned aircraft. Drone use warning issued by Glasgow Prestwick Airport | Ayr Advertiser
